title: `podman machine start` fails because it could not transition into a running state.
body:
### Issue Description

Whenever I create a new podman machine and run it, I always receive the following error:

```log
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
```

I tried removing all files in `~/.config/containers` and `~/.local/share/containers/podman`. That didn't help.

I tried forcefully setting the connection using `podman system connection default podman-machine-default`. That didn't help either.

I tried using podman from my system package manager (DNF) instead of Homebrew. This did nothing and was completely useless.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a new podman machine using `podman machine init`
2. Run `podman machine start`
3. Receive an error with an exit code of 125

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e9d8ca3-3992-4505-804d-5c8f7c7d2885)


### Describe the results you received

The podman machine could not transition to a running state because it is not connected to the configured ssh port.

This is what I interpreted from the error log.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected a message indicating that the machine successfully started. Such as,

```log
'podman-machine-default' was started successfully!
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes (with Homebrew)

### Additional environment details

I am running this on my own laptop. No virtualization whatsoever.

The laptop I am using is the **MSI Modern 15 A11M**.

### Additional information

It worked fine earlier when I was in university. When I had to go home, I put my laptop on hibernate. After I got home, I started my laptop, and then tried running the command `podman machine start` only for it to fail because of the aforementioned I described previously in this post.

comments.body: @FlawlessCasual17 please add the flag `--log-level=debug` to the `podman machine start` command and share the output. We will have more information to troubleshoot your problem.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

This also causes a QEMU window to pop up while this command runs.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3551d6b9-c67f-459b-9dfe-644cd475f679)

@l0rd, I no longer have this issue on Nobara Linux 42.

I believe when I checked for system updates and installed them, those system updates must have inadvertently also solved this problem related to podman.

I tested this using Podman from my system package manager. It worked.

I was also testing using Podman from Homebrew, which also worked.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/afb2b4c8-1965-47aa-b3c1-3a595f97e80f)
